I don't want to do a launch just to make some money from some guy. If it makes strategic sense, yes. So working with Glaser Kennedy, did that make strategic sense? Yes. To be able to say that I basically consulted with them or they were one of my clients, huge benefits. So Bill wanted me to run this launch. I said, hey Bill, tell me what, if you were to take this product, it was Bill's copywriting course, awesome course, online course. If you were going to roll this thing out on your own, what kind of sales would you do? Just if you're going to mail out to your list, the way you would do a normal promotion your list, it's $100,000. I'm like, cool. That's awesome. I wouldn't it be nice to just be able to send an email out and make a 100 grand from a product. I mean, that's a power, power, power list. So I took him at face value and Bill's like a very straight up guy. I said, okay, how about this? We'll do a launch, we'll use your list, we'll try to bring in some partners. What we'll do is, first 100 grand is yours, and once we get beyond 100 grand, you give me X percent. I think it was 10 percent. I'm not that cheap anymore, but I think it was 10 percent because I did. I wanted the feather in my cap. So how can you pass up on that? He gets all the money he would normally make, plus he gets 90 percent of everything on the upside. So they said yes, and we did it. I think the sales were somewhere between $700 and $750,000. So it worked out. It worked out. It was okay. So I've got a question for you. I see I even wrote question to remind me to ask you this question. At this point, how many of you would be interested in me running a launch for you? Could you raise your hands up high? Okay. That's the typical reaction. A lot of people are interested in me running launches. The problem is, I got no interest in doing it. I don't want to run your launches. I want to focus on my business. I care about my business a lot more than I care about your business. I'm sorry. All your product launch formulas are out. I love my product launch formula owners. You guys are awesome. You're my favorite people. Absolutely. But in reality, if I'm going to put in the work to do a launch, I want to get all the benefit. Because as you saw with Andrew, the benefit isn't just the dollars. I might do a launch for someone and only get, maybe I get 15 percent or 20 percent. Some of these launches I've done, I've done over $3 million. So 15 percent of $3 million, a lot of money, $450,000. Not too many consultants are getting paid $450,000, especially for working for three or four weeks. But the benefits that launch goes so far beyond that, there's the list build, there's the positioning, there's all the new buyers that you can sell additional stuff to, there's all the momentum. It's just a lot of reciprocal benefits, very powerful. I'd rather have all those benefits in my business. If I'm going to work on a launch, I'd rather have it be my launch. So lots and lots and lots of people wanted to hire me. In fact, my brother, who's in my business, he's a partner in a portion of it, he works for me in a portion of it. For a while, it was almost his full-time job just to say no to people. I'm just like a nice guy. I have a hard time saying no. I'm actually defenseless here. You usually want to go to events, I bring my brother just because he makes people feel so good when he says no. He's a nice guy too, but he's much better at saying no than I am. So that was his full-time job, literally. Because I'd have very influential people that I wanted to maintain my relationship with, so I didn't want to just blow them off. So I sent them to John and John blew them off in a very nice and elegant fashion. So anyways, lots and lots of people wanted to hire me. These clients had paid me a ton of money, but I was over it. They were happy to pay me tons of money, but I just could not handle the deal flow. It's just crazy. I guarantee you, right now, I've told you, well, let me be explicit, I don't do this. I do not run launches for anyone. I do not do it. But I guarantee you, after I get off the stage, like six of you are going to come up and ask me, can you run my launch? It happens every time I get on stage. So I'm like, okay, I got this massive deal flow, and there's clearly value. I just don't want to do it. I don't want to work on other people's stuff. So that led me to two questions. How can I attract and train and groom some talent, some people that can do what I do, that can help me with these client launches? I wasn't sure if they would be employees or if they would be partners, or if there would be an agency type. I had no idea. I just asked this question, how can I get some talent to do this? Then how can I make money while I'm doing that? So those are the questions I started with. I went down a long road and I asked a lot of people their opinion. I know I talked to Evan Pagan for a long time. I'm pretty sure I talked to Bill for a long time about that. I asked a lot of people. I asked my mastermind groups, and I went through a bunch of different ideas and iterations. Talked to Jay Abraham. One of the nice things is, I tend to be, for some people, I don't like to run their launches, but I'm okay with helping them with their launches. I'm okay taking a phone call or if they go into a pre-launch, like Evan Pagan. I don't know if you guys know him, but he's a brilliant, brilliant marketer. He's doing somewhere between 20 and $30 million a year selling in a variety of markets completely online. He's typical, when he wanted that launch about, I don't know, four or five weeks ago, he gets a call from me and it's like, Evan, how can I help you? I watched that first video and I think, I like this, I didn't like this, you got to change this, do this. So a lot of people tend to get calls from me like that. So one of them was Jay Abraham and I gave him some help. What's really nice is that it ends up always coming back to you. So Jay gave me some, what I'm about to show you, he gave me some really great help. It didn't cost me $7,000 an hour. Okay. So the solution was what I call Product Launch Manager. That's what really what I'm here to talk about. This is basically, I put together a full-out end-to-end program to teach people how to be a consultant and run these launches.
